codeReview.summary: The feature implements AnthropicBackend::stream() with SSE streaming support. The stream() method was already present in the codebase from a prior session; this session fixed a clippy unused_assignments warning and updated the live streaming test to use stream() instead of falling back to chat(). All review criteria are satisfied.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The skillFeedback indicates the worker followed the rust-worker procedure correctly, but the skill file path ~/.factory/skills/rust-worker/SKILL.md does not exist. This could mean the skill was referenced incorrectly or the file was removed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: feature.skillName is 'rust-worker' but glob search for ~/.factory/skills/rust-worker/SKILL.md returns ENOENT

summary: PASS - The AnthropicBackend::stream() implementation is correct and complete. The stream() method returns the correct Stream type (Pin<Box<dyn Stream<Item = Result<StreamEvent, SwellError>> + Send>>), calls the Anthropic messages API with stream=true, correctly parses SSE events (content_block_delta/text_delta, message_delta, message_stop), mirrors the structure of OpenAIBackend::stream(), and the live streaming test now uses stream() instead of chat(). The session fixed a clippy warning by removing an unused variable. No code issues found.
